## New files in `work_pilot_008_with-memory/`

### `app.py`

```python
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from flask import Flask, jsonify, request

app = Flask(__name__)

_START = time.monotonic()

USERS = {
    "1": {"id": "1", "name": "Alice Example", "email": "alice@example.com", "role": "admin"},
    "2": {"id": "2", "name": "Bob Sample", "email": "bob@example.com", "role": "member"},
}

ORDERS = [
    {"id": "101", "user_id": "1", "item": "Widget A",    "total": 19.99, "created_at": ...},  # -2h
    {"id": "102", "user_id": "2", "item": "Gadget B",    "total": 49.95, "created_at": ...},  # -5h
    {"id": "103", "user_id": "1", "item": "Doohickey C", "total": 7.50,  "created_at": ...},  # -30h (outside 24h window)
]

@app.get("/healthz")
def healthz(): ...

@app.get("/users/<user_id>/profile")
def get_user_profile(user_id: str): ...

@app.get("/orders/recent")
def list_recent_orders(): ...
```

### `pyproject.toml`

```toml
[project]
name = "flask-api"
version = "0.1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = ["flask>=3.1.0"]
```

---

## Install & run

```bash
cd work_pilot_008_with-memory

# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Run the dev server
uv run flask --app app run --debug
```

> The project uses `uv` per workspace convention — no `pip` or `requirements.txt`.

---

## Endpoint reference

| Method | Path | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `GET` | `/healthz` | Returns `{status, uptime_seconds, timestamp}` |
| `GET` | `/users/<user_id>/profile` | 200 with user dict, or `{"error": "user not found"}` + 404 |
| `GET` | `/orders/recent` | Orders from last 24 h, newest first. `?limit=N` (default 20, max 100) |

**Example calls:**
```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/healthz
curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/users/1/profile
curl "http://127.0.0.1:5000/orders/recent?limit=5"
```

> Note: order `103` (`-30h`) is intentionally outside the 24-hour window, so `/orders/recent` returns only two results by default — handy for verifying the cutoff logic works.
